title: Danger Signs — Severe Pre-eclampsia (EMERGENCY)
clinical_focus: Severe pre-eclampsia with danger signs — emergency classification and management
key_observations: Largest delta observed — skill transforms an understated note into a full emergency protocol
With_skill correctly classifies as 'pre-eclampsia with severe features' using exact WHO taxonomy; without_skill uses vague 'pre-eclampsia' and 'high blood pressure'
With_skill provides specific MgSO4 loading/maintenance doses and antihypertensive protocols with targets; without_skill hedges with 'consider' language and no dosing
With_skill explicitly suspends routine ANC; without_skill includes routine supplementation section in an emergency note
With_skill tone is unmistakably urgent with formatting emphasis; without_skill reads as moderate-concern clinic note
With_skill includes comprehensive monitoring parameters, toxicity signs, deterioration watch list; without_skill has minimal monitoring detail
Without_skill's 'delivery may be needed depending on how things progress' is dangerously understated for a condition where delivery is the definitive treatment
